title: Segmentation fault in Vtop___024root__trace_init_sub__TOP__0
body:
Hi there!
When running a somehow fancy design with a not-fancy testbench with VCD traces, I occasionally get this segmentation fault:

```
Thread 1 "Vtop" received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
0x00005555555702d1 in Vtop___024root__trace_init_sub__TOP__0 (vlSelf=0x5555555d7780, tracep=0x5555555fe678) at Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:12
12          const int c = vlSymsp->__Vm_baseCode;
(gdb) bt
#0  0x00005555555702d1 in Vtop___024root__trace_init_sub__TOP__0 (vlSelf=0x5555555d7780, tracep=0x5555555fe678) at Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:12
#1  0x0000555555574a06 in Vtop___024root__trace_init_top (vlSelf=0x5555555d7780, tracep=0x5555555fe678) at Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:323
#2  0x00005555555599cd in trace_init (voidSelf=0x5555555d7780, tracep=0x5555555fe678, code=<optimized out>) at Vtop.cpp:120
#3  0x00005555555685db in VerilatedTrace<VerilatedVcd, VerilatedVcdBuffer>::traceInit (this=0x5555555fe678) at /path/to/include/verilated_trace.h:343
#4  0x000055555556a708 in VerilatedVcd::open (this=0x5555555fe678, filename=<optimized out>) at /path/to/include/verilated_vcd_c.cpp:123
#5  0x0000555555559fe2 in run_test (my_module=0x5555555d7600, simlen=50, trace_filename=...) at /usr/include/c++/10/bits/basic_string.h:2312
#6  0x0000555555559c65 in main (argc=<optimized out>, argv=<optimized out>, env=<optimized out>) at /path/to/tb/tb_base.cc:136
```

Here are the two violations found by Valgrind:
```
==1884367== Use of uninitialised value of size 8
==1884367==    at 0x1242D1: Vtop___024root__trace_init_sub__TOP__0(Vtop___024root*, VerilatedVcd*) (Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:12)
==1884367==    by 0x128A05: Vtop___024root__trace_init_top(Vtop___024root*, VerilatedVcd*) (Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:323)
==1884367==    by 0x10D9CC: trace_init(void*, VerilatedVcd*, unsigned int) (Vtop.cpp:120)
==1884367==    by 0x11C5DA: VerilatedTrace<VerilatedVcd, VerilatedVcdBuffer>::traceInit() (verilated_trace_imp.h:331)
==1884367==    by 0x11E707: VerilatedVcd::open(char const*) (verilated_vcd_c.cpp:123)
==1884367==    by 0x10DFE1: run_test(Vtop*, int, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >) (tb_base.cc:86)
==1884367==    by 0x10DC64: main (tb_base.cc:136)
==1884367== 
==1884367== Invalid read of size 4
==1884367==    at 0x1242D1: Vtop___024root__trace_init_sub__TOP__0(Vtop___024root*, VerilatedVcd*) (Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:12)
==1884367==    by 0x128A05: Vtop___024root__trace_init_top(Vtop___024root*, VerilatedVcd*) (Vtop__Trace__0__Slow.cpp:323)
==1884367==    by 0x10D9CC: trace_init(void*, VerilatedVcd*, unsigned int) (Vtop.cpp:120)
==1884367==    by 0x11C5DA: VerilatedTrace<VerilatedVcd, VerilatedVcdBuffer>::traceInit() (verilated_trace_imp.h:331)
==1884367==    by 0x11E707: VerilatedVcd::open(char const*) (verilated_vcd_c.cpp:123)
==1884367==    by 0x10DFE1: run_test(Vtop*, int, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >) (tb_base.cc:86)
==1884367==    by 0x10DC64: main (tb_base.cc:136)
==1884367==  Address 0x1c is not stack'd, malloc'd or (recently) free'd
```

Here are the sources.
[vcd_segfault.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13635067/vcd_segfault.zip)

Thanks!
Flavien
